title: Representing Text as a Condition
concept: To guide image generation, the input text must first be converted into a numeric representation the diffusion model can use as conditioning.

exercises.prompt: When you type 'a red cat' into an AI art app, what does the computer actually see first?

options.text: The literal letters and words, just like you typed them
Numbers that represent the meaning of your words
A picture of a red cat immediately

exercises.explain: Computers can't 'read' words the way we do. Before an AI can draw anything, your text has to be turned into numbers it can work with.

cards.title: How Does AI 'Understand' Your Words?
cards.body: You type 'a dragon flying over mountains' and a picture appears. But computers only understand numbers! So how does your sentence turn into an image? The secret first step: turning your text into a special numeric code called a condition.

cards.title: Text as a Condition
cards.body: A 'condition' is a numeric representation of your text prompt. It captures the meaning of your words as a list of numbers. The image-generating AI (a diffusion model) reads this numeric code to know what to draw, instead of reading raw letters.

cards.title: Like a Musical Score
cards.body: A musician can't 'see' emotions on a page, but a musical score turns feelings into notes they can play. Similarly, an AI can't 'see' meaning in words directly, so your text prompt gets turned into a numeric 'score' the image model can follow to paint the picture.

cards.title: From Prompt to Picture
cards.body: You type: 'a happy dog in a garden.' First, a text encoder reads this and creates a list of numbers capturing 'happy,' 'dog,' and 'garden.' This numeric list is the condition. The diffusion model then uses this condition to guide every step of turning random noise into a matching image.

cards.title: Myth: AI Reads Words Like We Do
cards.body: Many think the AI 'reads' your sentence the way a person does. Truth: the AI never sees letters or grammar directly. It only sees numbers that represent the meaning. This numeric translation step is essential before any image can be generated.

cards.title: Text vs. Numeric Condition
cards.body: The same idea, in two very different forms — one for humans, one for machines.

left.text: 'a red cat sitting on a chair' — clear words with meaning to us

right.text: [0.12, -0.87, 0.45, ...] — a list of numbers the model uses to guide image creation

exercises.prompt: Why does text need to be converted into numbers before an image model can use it?

options.text: Because typing numbers is faster than typing words
Because diffusion models only process numeric representations, not raw text
Because numbers make the picture more colorful
Because it makes the prompt shorter

exercises.explain: Diffusion models work with numeric data. They can't directly interpret letters or grammar, so text must first be converted into a numeric condition.

exercises.prompt: The numeric condition captures the meaning of your text prompt, not just the letters.
exercises.explain: True! The conversion process focuses on capturing meaning (like 'happy,' 'dog,' 'garden') as numbers, not simply encoding individual letters.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Before a diffusion model can generate an image, the input text must be turned into a numeric ______ that represents its meaning.
exercises.explain: The numeric representation of your text is called a 'condition' because it conditions, or guides, the image generation process.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes what actually guides a diffusion model while it creates your image?

options.text: The raw sentence you typed, letter by letter
A numeric condition representing the meaning of your prompt
A random guess with no connection to your prompt

exercises.explain: The diffusion model uses the numeric condition, derived from your text's meaning, to steer the image generation process step by step.

exercises.prompt: A friend says, 'AI art generators just read your sentence like a person would, then paint it.' What's the best correction based on what you learned?

options.text: That's actually correct — the AI reads the sentence directly
Not quite — the AI first converts your text into a numeric condition representing its meaning, then uses that to guide image creation
The AI ignores the text completely and just guesses randomly
The AI turns your sentence into a sound file first

exercises.explain: The key concept: text prompts must be transformed into a numeric condition before any image generation can happen. This numeric form captures meaning, allowing the diffusion model to use it as guidance.
